domain_hint: Composite fuselage frame FEA stress analysis, ABAQUS/Standard, certification support with component-level validation discrepancy
ambiguity_notes: (1) Mesh refinement study report claims <2% difference between medium and fine meshes, but Appendix B tables show 7.3% for the safety-critical out-of-plane shear component — contradictory within the same document. (2) Model scope documentation (Sec 1.2) explicitly excludes fatigue use, but the fatigue team's current analysis plan lists this model as their stress input source — contradictory intended-use/scope evidence. (3) Rev C contact formulation change is described as improving theoretical fidelity, yet component-level validation shows Rev C has *worse* agreement with test data than Rev B — the rigor-improvement claim conflicts with the validation evidence. (4) The independent review memo concludes the model is "technically sound," but the reviewer explicitly did not examine the validation data comparisons or the boundary condition script — the stated conclusion overstates the scope of what was actually reviewed.
